scenario.name: creative
scenario.justification: This is the 'canary in the coal mine' for distributed inference. With 2048 autoregressive decode steps, the total latency added by network round-trips is: 2048 × RTT. For cross-continent (200ms RTT), that's 2048 × 0.2s = 409.6 seconds of PURE NETWORK WAITING. This mathematically proves why Tensor Parallelism across continents is architecturally impossible for real-time inference.

sensitivity.network: CRITICAL — Each of 2048 tokens requires GPU sync (AllReduce)
sensitivity.vram: LOW — Small input means minimal KV-cache

ttft.description: Time To First Token — measures prefill + scheduling latency

tpot.description: Time Per Output Token — steady-state decode speed

analysis.decode_bottleneck: COMPUTE/MEMORY-BOUND. GPU hardware is the primary bottleneck (healthy state).
